question: 1. What can travel the world while staying in one place?
2. What has a head and a tail but no body?
3. I live in your pocket but open the world. What am I?
hint: You tap on me all day.

question: 4. I go up high, but never leave the ground. What am I?
5. What has words but never speaks?
6. I’m not alive, but I can die. What am I?
7. You can hear me but not touch me, I can bring music or alarm. What am I?
8. The more I dry, the more I soak. What am I?
9. I come in pairs and help you go places, but I never walk. What am I?
10. I don’t speak but I tell stories, and I’m kept on a shelf. What am I?
11. What has a bark but no bite?
12. What is full of holes but still holds a lot?
13. What has to be broken before you can eat it?
14. What is white when dirty and clean when used?
15. You use me to eat but never eat me. What am I?
16. You see me once in a minute, twice in a moment, but never in a thousand years. What am I?
17. I have stripes but no color, pages but no words. What am I?
18. If you drop me, I’m sure to crack, but give me a smile and I’ll smile back. What am I?
19. I have four legs in the morning, two legs at noon, and three in the evening. What am I?
20. What can you catch, but not throw?
21. I follow you around, but I’m not your pet. I appear in the light but disappear in darkness. What am I?
22. What has a head, a tail, is brown, and has no legs?
23. You can see me in the water, but I never get wet. I move when you move. What am I?
24. What can be caught but never thrown?
25. Every time you say my name, I disappear a little more. I'm something people often want but cannot maintain. What am I?
26. What belongs to you but others use it more than you do?
27. What can you break, even if you never touch it?
28. I’m always moving forward but never leave my place. What am I?
29. I go up but never down. What am I?
30. What goes around the world but stays in the same spot?
31. What can you catch but not throw?
32. The more of me you take, the bigger I get. What am I?
33. I have a heart that doesn’t beat. What am I?
34. I have no lungs but I need air. What am I?
35. I don’t have a mouth, but I roar. What am I?
36. What can fly without wings and cry without eyes?
37. What runs but never walks, has a bed but never sleeps, and has a mouth but never eats?
38. What has keys but can’t open doors?
39. What can you fill with empty hands?
40. What invention lets you look through walls?
41. I get smaller every time you use me. What am I?
42. I’m a box that holds keys without locks, yet they can unlock your deepest thoughts. What am I?
43. I get bigger the more you take away from me. What am I?
44. You throw away the outside, cook the inside, then eat the outside and throw away the inside. What is it?
45. I’m full of holes but still holds water. What am I?
46. I go around all the places yet always stay in a corner. What am I?
47. Though I move without legs and see without eyes, I’m trusted by all. I speak in signals and silence. What am I?
hint: It’s in your hand right now.

question: 48. What has a head, a tail, but no body?
49. I can be cracked, made, and told. What am I?
50. What grows when shared and dies when kept?
51. I fill up a room but take no space. What am I?
52. I’m light as a whisper but strong enough to topple kings. What am I?
53. I am not alive, but I grow. I do not have lungs, but I need air. What am I?
54. What has teeth but cannot bite?
hint: You use me on your hair.

question: 55. What can be opened but never closed again?
56. What has a face and two hands, but no arms or legs?
57. I am the cage that opens when you enter. What am I?
58. You see a boat filled with people. It hasn’t sunk, but when you look again you don’t see a single person on the boat. Why?
59. You cannot touch me, but I move you. I can cause a tear, a smile, or goosebumps. I’m heard, not seen. What am I?
hint: You feel me in your chest sometimes.

question: 60. I have four fingers and a thumb, but I’m not alive. What am I?
hint: You wear me on your hand.

question: 61. What can you serve but never eat?
62. What belongs to you but is used more by others?
63. I'm taken from a mine and shut in a wooden case. Used by students daily. What am I?
64. What comes after thunder but before silence?
65. I can destroy kingdoms or build empires. I exist in every form of life, yet you can’t hold me. What am I?
hint: Often stored in books and brains.

question: 66. What has legs but doesn't walk?
67. I am the tide that rises with giving. What am I?
68. I come after thunder, but before the calm. I flash across the sky but don’t make a sound. What am I?
69. I’m something people work for all their lives, yet I’m not a thing you can touch. I often come too late, or not at all. What am I?
70. The more you take from me, the more I grow. What am I?
71. What flies without wings and cries without eyes?
72. What’s in front of you but can’t be seen?
73. I am not alive, but I can grow. I don’t have lungs, but I need air. What am I?
hint: I consume everything in my path if uncontrolled.

question: 74. I have hands but no arms, face but no eyes, and I always tell the truth. What am I?
75. I am the bridge you build with two hands. What am I?
76. What can fill a room but takes up no space?
77. I have pages but no cover, numbers but no math, and I help you measure passing time. What am I?
hint: Used every day of the year.

question: 78. I can bring memories to life without saying a word. I freeze a moment in time and hold it forever. What am I?
79. What never asks a question but gets answered all the time?
80. I get louder the more you hush me. What am I?
81. I can bring tears to your eyes, resurrect the dead, make you smile, and reverse time. What am I?
82. The more you look at me, the less you see. What am I?
83. I’m tall when I’m young and short when I’m old. What am I?
84. What has many needles but doesn't sew?
85. I can be cracked, made, told, and sometimes played. Though I'm intangible, I can make people laugh. What am I?
86. The more of them you take, the more you leave behind. What are they?
87. You can’t hold me, but I can trap you. I’m invisible yet can spread faster than any flame. What am I?
88. What has teeth but can't bite?
89. I am always in front of you but can’t be seen. What am I?
90. The more you press me, the more I move. What am I?
91. You can catch me but cannot throw me. What am I?
92. I turn once, what is out will not get in. I turn again, what is in will not get out. What am I?
hint: I control access with just a twist.

question: 93. I am the lock that opens by forgetting. What am I?
94. I appear during the night and vanish by morning. I may tell you stories, but they’re never real. What am I?
hint: Your mind creates me while you sleep.

question: 95. What goes through cities and fields but never moves?
96. What has a thumb and four fingers but is not alive?
97. You can open me but you can’t close me. I’m found on your face. What am I?
98. The more you press me, the louder I get. What am I?
99. I have a spine but no bones, pages but no face. What am I?
100. What can be cracked, made, told, and played?
101. What has one eye but can't see?
102. I am the beginning of the end and the end of time and space. What am I?
103. What can run but never walks, has a bed but never sleeps, and has a mouth but never talks?
104. What flies forever, rests never?
105. What can travel around the world while staying in the same corner?
106. I am heavy forward, but backward I am not. What am I?
107. I am the compass that points to your heart. What am I?
108. I don’t move, but I grow; I may crack but never bleed. I’m often found in landscapes. What am I?
109. What can fill a room but doesn’t take up any space?
110. I’m round, used in sports, and bounce all over. What am I?
111. I have hands, but I cannot clap. What am I?
112. What is always old and sometimes new, never sad, sometimes blue, never empty, sometimes full, never pushes, always pulls?
113. What belongs to you but others use it more than you?
114. What has a foot but no legs?
115. What gets lost every time you look for it, yet always returns?
116. You can’t see me, but I determine every second of your life. I’m not money, but more valuable. What am I?
hint: You lose me with every tick.

question: 117. I am the book that never ends but keeps starting. What am I?
118. I am the fire that cools hearts. What am I?
119. I have lakes with no water, mountains with no stone, and cities with no buildings. What am I?
120. I go around all the places, cities, towns, and villages, but always stay in one corner. What am I?
121. Though I’m not alive, I grow. I start small, and with enough time, I become towering and ancient. What am I?
122. The more I dry, the wetter I become. What am I?
123. I am the letter that starts and ends silence. What am I?
124. I’m not alive, but I grow. I don’t have lungs, but I need air. I don’t have a mouth, and yet I drown. What am I?
125. What has an eye but cannot see and is associated with a storm?
126. What is always coming but never arrives?
127. You see me once in June, twice in November, but not at all in May. What am I?
128. What’s bought by the yard but worn by the foot?
129. What has a ring but no finger?
130. I am the mountain made of minutes. What am I?
131. What can be broken, but is never held?
132. What can you hold in your left hand but not in your right?
133. The more you take, the more you leave behind. What am I?
134. I am found in the sky but not in space. I often bring rain, but I’m made of water vapor. What am I?
135. I have no doors, but I can open wide. I have no mouth, yet I can make a breeze. What am I?
136. I’m clean when I’m black, dirty when I’m white. What am I?
137. What is so fragile that saying its name breaks it?
138. I am the mirror that shows what you could be. What am I?
139. I am the bell you hear after a long climb. What am I?
140. I am the mirror that makes you older and wiser in seconds. What am I?
141. What can't talk but will reply when spoken to?
142. I’m a path that never bends, yet leads to both ends. What am I?
143. I can be long or short, I can be grown or bought. I can be painted or left bare. I can be round or square. What am I?
144. You measure my life in hours, I serve you by expiring. I’m quick when I’m thin and slow when I’m fat. What am I?
145. What has a bed but doesn’t sleep, and runs but never walks?
146. I am light as a feather, yet the strongest person can’t hold me for more than five minutes. What am I?
147. What is made of water but if you put it into water it dies?
148. I exist only when shared. The moment I am spoken, I disappear. What am I?
149. What kind of band doesn’t play music?
150. What is always in front of you but can’t be seen?
151. I can hide mountains and cities alike, covering all in my path, yet I am nothing but vapor. What am I?
152. I cannot be touched or held, yet I leave a mark. I am created in moments and remembered forever. What am I?
153. What breaks and never falls, and what falls and never breaks?
154. What can bring back the dead, make us cry, make us laugh, make us young? What is born in an instant yet lasts a lifetime?
hint: You relive me in your mind.

question: 155. I am always ahead of you but never in sight. What am I?
156. I am always moving but never leave my spot. I tick and tock but have no feet. What am I?
hint: I keep track of every second.

question: 157. The more you take me, the more you leave behind. What am I?
158. I am the shadow that never leaves your side. What am I?
159. What kind of tree can you carry in your hand?
160. The more of me you see, the less you see. What am I?
161. I am the leaf that never falls. What am I?
162. I’m invisible but weigh heavily. I’m carried in silence, but shared in whispers. What am I?
163. You see me in the sky but never touch me. I paint the world but vanish in the dark. What am I?
164. I start with a tick and end with a tock, measuring every passing second even though you never see me move. What am I?
hint: You always run out of me.

question: 165. Though I have no tongue, I always reply when spoken to.
166. I am the ocean contained in a tear. What am I?
167. The more you take from me, the more I become. I start as nothing, but end up as space. What am I?
168. What has many teeth but can’t bite?
169. What has cities, but no houses; forests, but no trees; and rivers, but no water?
170. I have no voice, yet I can teach. I have no mouth, yet I can speak. What am I?
171. I have no life, but I can die if dropped. What am I?
172. What is made of water but if you put it into water it will die?
173. What runs but never moves, has a head but no body, and gurgles with life?
174. I am full of words, yet I cannot speak. I hold information, but you must open me to find it. What am I?
175. I am the river that runs through books. What am I?
176. Though I have no life, I help bring life. You bury me to let others grow. What am I?
177. I am always right but never correct. I lead you but have no legs. What am I?
178. What word is spelled incorrectly in every dictionary?
179. I come at night but disappear with the sun. What am I?
180. I am shaped by time and forged by experience. I grow with you, and yet I can be lost in a moment. What am I?
181. What has four wheels and flies?
182. I brighten the sky but disappear at night. What am I?
183. What gets sharper the more you use it?
184. The more you use me, the smaller I get. What am I?
185. What has roots as nobody sees, is taller than trees, up, up it goes, and yet never grows?
186. Though I exist in every place, I am often taken for granted. I can be polluted, yet I’m needed to live. What am I?
187. Though I’m not a bird, I can fly through the sky. I’m not a river, but I’m full of current. What am I?
188. You buy me to eat, but never eat me. What am I?
189. What comes once in a minute, twice in a moment, but never in a thousand years?
190. What wears a cap but has no head?
191. What gets bigger the more you take away from it?
192. I am taken from a mine and shut inside a box. People use me every day. What am I?
193. What has a neck but no head, two arms but no hands?
194. What has many keys but can’t open locks?
195. I’m never the same for two people. I depend on belief, perception, and choice. I can save or destroy. What am I?
hint: What you see may not be the full picture.

question: 196. I am invisible, but I can carry messages across the world in a blink. What am I?
197. What can run but never walks, has a bed but never sleeps, has a mouth but never talks?
198. You cannot touch me, yet I touch you daily. I guide your routine and schedule your life. What am I?
199. I exist where no one can see, felt only through consequence. I balance all but ask for nothing. What am I?
200. What has 13 hearts but no other organs?
201. I am the light that never shines, the sound that never speaks. What am I?
202. The more you know me, the less I exist. What am I?
203. Though I am invisible, I can make you move, I can break you down, and I can build you up. What am I?
204. What can be touched but can’t be seen, broken but never held?
205. What goes up but never comes down?
206. What has to be broken before you can use it?
207. I’m tall when I’m young, and short when I’m old. What am I?
208. You see a boat filled with people, but there isn’t a single person on board. How?
209. I am the garden you plant without seeds. What am I?
210. The more you try to grasp me, the less of me you have. I vanish when observed. What am I?
211. I do not have a mouth, yet I can speak. I tell truths and lies alike, and I live in the hands of many. What am I?
hint: You turn my pages for answers.

question: 212. What has an eye but cannot see?
213. What has a tongue but can't talk?
214. I rise each morning without fail, chase away shadows, and retreat each night. What am I?
215. What has a heart that doesn’t beat?
216. What has eyes but can’t see and is full of holes?
217. What belongs to you, but other people use it more than you do?
218. What can be broken without being held?
219. What can you break, even if you never pick it up or touch it?
220. I’m in your pocket, on your wrist, on your wall, yet I don’t exist at all. What am I?
hint: Everyone runs out of me eventually.

question: 221. I can be cracked, made, told, and played. What am I?
222. What can be touched but can’t be seen, and can break without being held?
223. I am the only thing you can’t see until it’s too late. I shape your present and was once your future. What am I?
224. What can you throw but not catch?
225. I’m taken from a mine, shut inside a wooden case, never released, and yet I’m used by everyone. What am I?
226. I have keys but no locks, I have space but no room. You can enter but can’t go outside. What am I?
227. I’m white when I’m dirty and black when I’m clean. What am I?
228. What has ears but can’t hear?
229. I fly without wings, cry without eyes. Wherever I go, darkness flies. What am I?
230. I have a body but no bones, I run but have no legs, and I go on forever without a single breath. What am I?
hint: You’ll find me on maps and landscapes.

question: 231. I precede all kings but wear no crown. I mark beginnings and yet am never first. What am I?
232. What has one eye but cannot see?
hint: The calmest part is in the center.

question: 233. What can travel the world while staying in the same spot?
234. What has a neck but no head?
235. What has rooms but no doors, floors but no tiles, and walls but no bricks?
236. I am born when you speak, yet I die with silence. What am I?
hint: You’ll hear me reply, but never see me.

question: 237. I cross cities and towns, but I never move. I guide people from place to place without speaking a word. What am I?
hint: You drive or walk on me every day.

question: 238. I’m hot in the day but cool at night. I shine without flame. What am I?
239. I am the first to leave and the last to arrive; I make beginnings from endings. What am I?
240. I can surround you but remain unseen. I can lift or crush without touch. What am I?
241. I may seem like nothing, yet I make up everything. I’m found in space, in matter, and in silence. What am I?
hint: Too small to see, but everywhere.

question: 242. I build empires, yet I’m invisible. I fuel wars and peace alike. I’m not a person, yet I rule many. What am I?
243. I fly high, make noise, carry weight, yet I never flap my wings. What am I?
244. What runs on tracks, never leaves its place, but still moves people far and wide?
245. I’m hard as rock, but made of air. I fall from the sky, but vanish on touch. What am I?
246. I am the wind that teaches leaves to let go. What am I?
247. I am the book that writes itself. What am I?
248. I have cities but no houses, forests but no trees. What am I?
249. What is full of holes but still holds water?
250. I exist only when there is light, but direct light kills me. What am I?
251. I can cross any border, start a revolution, or simply change one person. I begin with just a few letters. What am I?
252. I fall but never rise. I cover the ground in white. What am I?
253. I have no wings, but I can fly through the sky. I cause damage but I’m not alive. What am I?
254. What kind of ship has two mates but no captain?
255. I am the path that opens when you admit you’re lost. What am I?
256. The more you take from me, the bigger I get. What am I?
257. What can travel around the world while staying in a corner?
258. What kind of coat is always wet when you put it on?
259. The more you feed me, the hungrier I become. What am I?
260. I rise without a flame, make no noise, and disappear in the air. What am I?
261. I am born when two mirrors face each other. I reflect what reflects me, endlessly. What am I?
262. I shrink smaller every time you sharpen me. What am I?
263. I may not speak, but I can make you cry or laugh. I can be written, spoken, or sung. What am I?
264. I am the mirror that reflects without judgment. What am I?
265. I am the seed that asks for rain. What am I?
266. I exist in every country, language, and culture. I can’t be seen, but I can be felt. What am I?
hint: Love, fear, joy — all fall under me.

question: 267. What flattens all mountains, wipes out all species, destroy every building, and turns everything into pieces?
268. Though I don’t speak, I can answer questions. Though I don’t have eyes, I help you see the world. What am I?
269. What has a thumb and four fingers but isn't alive?
270. What has pages but isn’t a book?
271. What can run, but never walks; has a bed, but never sleeps; has a mouth, but never talks?
272. I'm flat as paper but carry whole worlds inside me. What am I?
273. I’m not alive, but I can grow. I don’t have senses, but I can respond. I may live in labs and computers. What am I?
hint: I'm answering your riddles right now.

question: 274. I can be hot or cold, fast or slow. You control me with the turn of a handle, yet I can surprise you. What am I?
hint: You meet me in the shower.

question: 275. What has an endless supply of letters but starts empty?
276. I’m a weightless crown, yet kings fear me. What am I?
277. I am where you go to find yourself, but also where you go to escape. I can be real or imagined. What am I?
hint: Open me and enter another world.

question: 278. I am the ruler that measures without numbers. What am I?
279. I go up but never come down. What am I?
280. I’m a bridge you can’t walk on, a link you can’t hold. What am I?
281. What can run but never walks, has a mouth but never talks, and has a head but never thinks?
282. What goes up and down but never moves?
283. What gets broken without being held?
284. I’m always in front of you but can’t be seen. What am I?
285. I am the wound that grows with every bandage. What am I?
286. Remove one letter and I remain the same. Remove two and I’m still unchanged. What word am I?
287. You can hold me but not touch me. I can be strong or weak. What am I?
288. You can find me in the sky or at the bottom of the sea. I am blue. What am I?
289. The more you remove from me, the stronger I become. I need emptiness to thrive. What am I?
290. What has no eyes, no ears, no mouth, yet sees and hears all?
291. What has a spine but no bones?
292. What is always hungry and must be fed or it dies?
293. I can’t be seen, but I can fill a room. I’m not heard, but I can break the silence. What am I?
294. What is easy to get into but hard to get out of?
295. I get smaller every time I’m used. What am I?
296. Though I am not a person, I am named, celebrated, and waited for all year long. What am I?
hint: Once a year, just for you.

question: 297. I am the path that leads nowhere but everywhere. What am I?
298. What can you hold without ever touching it?
299. I am lighter than a feather, but not even the world’s strongest man can hold me for long.
300. I can’t be held or seen, yet I shape every conversation. I connect people without being a person. What am I?
hint: It’s what you’re reading right now.

question: 301. You see a boat filled with people, yet there isn’t a single person on board. How is that possible?
302. You can cut me without a knife and tie me without string. What am I?
303. What has one eye but can’t see?
304. I don’t have wings, but I can fly. I don’t have eyes, but I will cry! What am I?
305. I am both a question and an answer. I am often sought but not always found. I can give meaning or confusion. What am I?
306. I start out tall, but the longer I stand, the shorter I grow. What am I?
307. I have wings but I’m not alive. I help things fly. What am I?
308. I can be long or short; I can be grown or bought; I can be painted or left bare. What am I?
309. Forward I’m heavy, backward I’m not. What am I?
310. The more you remove from me, the clearer I become. What am I?
311. What has no beginning, middle, or end?
312. Which word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
313. What disappears the moment you say its name?
314. The more you take away from me, the larger I become. What am I?
315. What disappears as soon as you say its name?
316. Though I never sleep or eat, I am always full. I can store anything, yet I am nothing without others. What am I?
317. The more you remove from me, the bigger I get. What am I?
318. What has a head but never weeps, has a bed but never sleeps?
319. I am not alive, but I grow. I have no lungs, but I need air. What am I?
320. I am often mistaken for being small, yet I carry the universe in me. What am I?
321. I’m full of holes but I can still hold water. What am I?
322. I make things visible but cannot be seen myself. What am I?
323. I speak without a voice, I guide without a hand. What am I?
324. What can run but never walks, has a bed but never sleeps?
325. What comes at the end of everything?
326. I am seen in the water but never get wet. What am I?
327. I go in dry and come out wet. The longer I stay in, the stronger my scent. What am I?
328. I’m present in war and peace, in joy and sorrow. I shape your memories and define your future. What am I?
hint: You never have enough of me.

question: 329. I go in hard, come out soft, and am never the same. What am I?
330. I am the candle that burns without flame. What am I?
331. Though I belong to you, others use me more. I’m part of your identity and yet spoken by strangers. What am I?
hint: You likely hear me every day.

question: 332. What begins with T, ends with T, and has T inside it?
333. I move silently without a trace, follow you everywhere, and disappear in darkness. What am I?
hint: Always at your side — or behind you.

question: 334. A paradox by nature, I am both everything and nothing. I am the container and the contents. What am I?
hint: I’m a number, or am I?

question: 335. I am stronger than steel, but invisible. I can lift or crush without a sound. What am I?
hint: You can feel me, but never catch me.

question: 336. You’ll find me once in a second, twice in a decade, but never in a moment. What am I?
337. I am not born, yet I die. I never move, but I can be lost. I hold value beyond gold. What am I?
338. I come before morning and after night, but I’m not part of the day. What am I?
339. I hold a million mouths but never taste. What am I?
340. I go in dry and come out wet. The longer I’m in, the stronger I get. What am I?
341. I eat the future and leave the past. What am I?
342. I am the silence that says more than words. What am I?
343. If you drop me I’m sure to crack, but give me a smile and I’ll smile back. What am I?
344. What belongs to you but other people use it more than you do?
345. I’m invisible, and without me, you can’t live. What am I?
346. I go in hard and come out soft. You blow me to cool down. What am I?
347. What has four legs in the morning, two at noon, and three in the evening?
348. I only come out at night without being called, and I'm lost in the day without being stolen. What am I?
349. What can fly without wings?
350. The more you take away, the more I appear. What am I?
351. What has one letter but starts and ends with an envelope?
352. I am the road that returns you to yourself. What am I?
353. I am the key that opens every door but never leaves a mark. What am I?
354. I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have no body, but I come alive with wind. What am I?
355. What runs but never walks and has a mouth but never talks?
356. The more you look at me, the more I vanish. What am I?
357. I can make you laugh, make you cry, even change your mind. I am everywhere and nowhere. What am I?
hint: Humans have told me for thousands of years.

question: 358. I can fill a room but take up no space. What am I?
359. I am invisible, but I affect everyone. I am the reason for change and progress. What am I?
360. I have a thousand wheels but never move. What am I?
361. I am an ocean without water, a forest without trees, and a desert without sand. What am I?
362. What has a face and two hands but no arms or legs?
363. I am the mountain moved by a whisper. What am I?
364. What can go through glass without breaking it?
365. What has ears but cannot hear?
366. What gets louder the more you silence it?
367. No matter how much you feed me, I grow weaker. What am I?
368. What has a face that doesn’t frown, hands that don’t move, and runs but stays in place?
369. If you have me, you want to share me. If you share me, you don’t have me. What am I?
370. What has hands but can’t clap?
371. What has roots but never grows?
hint: It stands tall but never moves.

question: 372. I am the lock unlocked by a promise. What am I?
373. What is easy to lift but hard to throw far?
374. I connect past and present, fact and feeling. I am vivid, sometimes false, and always personal. What am I?
375. I can be long or short; I can be grown or bought; I can be painted or left bare; I can be round or square. What am I?
hint: Found on fingers or in hardware.

question: 376. I have a neck but no head. What am I?
377. I am something you need every day, yet you can’t hold me in your hands. I can bring comfort or stress. What am I?
hint: Too little makes you tired, too much makes you lazy.

question: 378. I appear in the dark, often in the sky, sometimes a sliver, sometimes whole. I pull the oceans and inspire wolves. What am I?
hint: You can’t see me during a new phase.

question: 379. I come out at night without being called, and I disappear by day without being stolen. What am I?
380. The more you stretch me, the thinner I become. The more I’m used, the more I vanish. What am I?
hint: Snap me and I might break.

question: 381. I speak all languages, but utter no sound. I cross all borders, but carry no passport. What am I?
382. What has no doors but has keys and can make music?
383. I travel the world without leaving my home. I am filled with voices but never speak. What am I?
hint: Tuned in, but never tuned out.

question: 384. I have stripes but no color, pages but no stories. What am I?
385. I go around all day, always returning to where I began. I carry the world on my back. What am I?
386. I am the currency of favors. What am I?
387. I disappear every time you say my name. What am I?
388. I don’t make a sound, but I’m known by all. I have no shape, yet I shape your path. I am feared and admired. What am I?
hint: I can’t be stopped or reversed.

question: 389. What invention lets you look through a wall?
390. Though I cannot move or breathe, I hold the world within me. I’m filled with places, borders, and names. What am I?
hint: You use me to travel without moving.

question: 391. I’m the result of thought, the product of silence, and often the beginning of change. What am I?
392. What can you hold in your right hand but never in your left hand?
393. I am the signal that waves without hands. What am I?
394. What goes through towns and over hills but never moves?
395. I have no eyes, no legs, or ears, and yet I move the earth. What am I?
396. What goes up and down without moving?
397. I am shared by lovers, broken by lies, and treasured for a lifetime. I have no weight, but I can be heavy. What am I?
398. I come after thunder but before calm. I can frighten or soothe, and I’m often followed by a rainbow. What am I?
hint: I fall, but I never rise.

question: 399. What comes once in a year, twice in a month, four times in a week, and six times in a day?
400. What flies without wings and strikes without hands?
401. I’m a place without doors or windows, but full of stories. What am I?
402. I wear a crown but am no king. I can cause drought or flood. What am I?
403. You answer me, although I never ask you questions. What am I?
404. I have cities, but no houses. I have mountains, but no trees. I have water, but no fish. What am I?
405. I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. What am I?
406. What is made of water but can’t be touched?
407. I’m hard as rock, but melt in your mouth. What am I?
408. I make two people out of one. What am I?
409. I divide yet unite. I separate yet bind. I am found on faces but also in time. What am I?
410. I’m always in a corner but never in the room. What am I?
411. What is always in front of you but can't be seen?
412. What has four legs in the morning, two in the afternoon, and three in the evening?
413. I’m found in your room but never used to clean. What am I?
414. I break without being touched, vanish when shared, and grow in silence. What am I?
415. What runs all around a backyard yet never moves?
416. When I am measured, I vanish. When I am spoken, I die. What am I?
417. What has no bones, but can be broken?
418. I am taken from a mine and shut in a wooden case, from which I am never released, and yet I am used by almost every person. What am I?
419. What can’t talk but will reply when spoken to?
420. I’m always running but never walk. I have a bed but never sleep. What am I?
421. The more you have of me, the less you see. What am I?
422. No matter how little or how much you use me, you change me every month. What am I?
hint: I track what passes but never moves.

question: 423. I go up when the rain comes down. What am I?
424. I’m taken from a mine and shut in a wooden case, never released, and yet I am used by almost every person. What am I?
425. You can serve me but never eat me. What am I?
426. I am the silence that speaks volumes. What am I?
427. I shrink smaller every time you use me, yet I help you stay clean. What am I?
428. What can't be used until it's broken?
429. I am taken once a day, yet I’m not medicine. I involve thought, but not action. What am I?
430. You throw away the outside, cook the inside, eat the outside, and throw away the inside. What is it?
431. I’m a word of letters three, add two and fewer there will be. What am I?
hint: A play on word and count.

question: 432. I am always hungry, I must always be fed. The finger I touch, will soon turn red. What am I?
hint: Feed me wood or I die.

question: 433. What gets bigger the more you take away?
434. What comes before winter and after summer?
435. I have roots but never grow. I have a trunk but no leaves. What am I?
436. What has no beginning, end, or middle?
437. What comes up but never comes down?
438. I'm tall when I'm young and short when I'm old. What am I?
439. What’s always in the past, never in the future?
440. What doesn’t speak but tells you everything?
441. The more you pull me, the shorter I get. What am I?
442. What can fill your pocket without taking any space?
443. What is bought by the yard and worn by the foot?
444. I am the castle built from small bricks called days. What am I?
445. I’m a king’s toy, a child’s delight, and a scholar’s foe. What am I?
446. What can travel without wheels and flies without wings?
447. I am the ruler that measures stories, not inches. What am I?
448. I am the garment of words that fits every occasion. What am I?
449. I'm light as a feather but the strongest man can't hold me for more than 5 minutes. What am I?
450. I am the mine whose gems are questions. What am I?
451. I can only live where there is light, but I die if the light shines on me. What am I?
452. What breaks but never falls, and what falls but never breaks?
453. The more you feed me, the more I grow. If you give me water, I die. What am I?
454. What can you hold in your right hand but never in your left?
455. I am a word of letters three, add two and fewer there will be. What am I?
456. I’m lighter than air but can’t be lifted by hands. What am I?
457. I move without legs, flutter without wings. I may whisper, I may roar, but I have no voice to sing. What am I?
hint: You can feel me, but not see me.

question: 458. I have no color, yet I'm the artist's canvas. I am empty but full of possibility. What am I?
459. I fly without wings, I cry without eyes. Wherever I go, darkness flies. What am I?
460. The more you have of it, the less you see. What is it?
461. I’m not alive, but I grow. I don’t have lungs, but I need air. What am I?
462. I am not alive, but I can grow; I don’t have lungs, but I need air. What am I?
463. I have no flesh, no feathers, no scales, no bones. Yet I have fingers and thumbs of my own. What am I?
hint: Worn in cold or during work.

question: 464. If you drop me I’ll crack, but give me a smile and I’ll smile back. What am I?
465. I’m not alive, but I can grow. I don’t have lungs, but I need air. What am I?
466. I run all around a backyard, yet I never move. What am I?
467. I am the coat that protects by revealing. What am I?
468. What can you keep after giving to someone?
469. I am weightless, but you can’t hold me for long. What am I?
470. I am the treasure that can’t be bought, only earned. What am I?
471. I am the flame that warms without smoke. What am I?
472. What comes before thunder?
473. What goes up when rain comes down?
474. I have wings and can fly, but I am not a bird. I have a tail, but I am not a fish. What am I?
hint: You use a string to fly it.

question: 475. I’m a box with keys but no lid, locks, or code. What am I?
476. I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have no body, but I come alive with wind.
477. What has no life but can die if not fed?
478. What comes once in a year, twice in a week, but never in a day?
479. What has one voice, goes on four legs in the morning, two legs at noon, and three legs in the evening?
480. I am the garden that grows from questions. What am I?
481. I get wetter the more I dry. What am I?
hint: You use me after a shower.

question: 482. I have cities but no houses, forests but no trees, and rivers but no water. What am I?
483. What five-letter word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
484. What has ears but never hears?
485. I begin with silence, grow with words, and end with noise. What am I?
486. I go through cities and fields but never move. What am I?
487. The more you fill me, the lighter I become. What am I?
488. What kind of coat can only be put on when it’s wet?
489. I am the stitch that binds strangers into a family. What am I?
490. I am the seed that becomes an idea in another mind. What am I?
491. What comes down but never goes up?
492. What has keys but can't open locks?
493. You can’t see me, but you feel me when you’re scared. What am I?
494. What has many keys but can't open a single lock?
495. I am the river that flows in circles. What am I?
496. I promise everything and cost nothing until taken. What am I?
497. What begins with an E but only contains one letter?
498. I'm invisible, I weigh nothing, but if you put me in a barrel, it becomes lighter. What am I?
499. I’m made of glass but show you the world. What am I?
500. What can you keep after giving it to someone else?
501. I'm not a plane, but I fly; I'm not a river, but I carry current. What am I?
502. What comes after lightning and before silence?
503. What gets used by everyone, even though it belongs to only one person?
504. I shrink smaller every time I take a bath. What am I?
505. I’m full of keys but open no doors. What am I?
506. I come in different colors, but I'm not a rainbow. I'm used to write or draw. What am I?
507. I’m cold, made of water, and crunch under your feet. What am I?
508. I don’t speak but I answer your questions. What am I?
509. What gets shorter as you use it?
510. The more you feed me, the hungrier I get. What am I?
511. I have hands but no arms, a face but no eyes. What am I?
512. The more you look at me, the less you can see. What am I?
513. I exist only when there’s light, but direct light kills me. What am I?
514. I run all around a backyard but never move. What am I?
515. What has a bank but no money?
516. What has a bottom at the top?
517. You can’t see me, but I’m around you always. I help you breathe.
518. I’m in the sky and I light up the night. I come out when it’s dark. What am I?
519. I am a path that never walks, a bed that never sleeps, and a bank that has no money. What am I?
520. I am an odd number. Take away a letter and I become even. What am I?
521. I shrink the more I wash. What am I?
522. What is always in front of you but you can’t see it?
523. I am always moving but never leave my spot. What am I?
524. What has many teeth but can't bite?
525. I have wings and I buzz, but I’m not a bird. What am I?
526. You’ll find me in the sky at night, sometimes full, sometimes new. What am I?
527. You buy me to eat but never eat me. What am I?
528. What gets wet while drying?
529. What can run, but never walks, has a mouth but never talks?
530. I go up and down but never move. What am I?
hint: You can feel me rise and fall.

question: 531. You wear me every day but never wash me. What am I?
532. What kind of band never plays music?
533. I am always in front of you but can never be seen. What am I?
534. The more you share me, the less you have. What am I?
535. What gets lost every time you stand still?
536. I am the echo of a promise. What am I?
537. I'm small enough to fit in your palm, yet I can hold the world. What am I?
hint: You might be using me right now.

question: 538. What word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
539. I’m used for comfort but can’t comfort you. I’m seen every night but never in the same shape. What am I?
540. I shrink smaller every time I take a bath, but I always clean. What am I?
541. What begins with T, ends with T, and has T in it?
542. I am used by the wise, feared by the foolish, and wasted by the idle. What am I?
543. You look through me to see the world clearly, but I must be clean to help. What am I?
544. I go up and down the stairs without moving. What am I?
545. Though I may fall from the sky and shimmer in the light, I melt away quickly and leave no trace. What am I?
hint: Each one of me is unique.

question: 546. I am the key that opens every heart but never leaves a mark. What am I?
547. What has no beginning, end, or middle but touches everything?
548. I am the shadow that guides your steps at night. What am I?
549. I am taken from a mine and shut in a wooden case, from which I am never released, and yet I am used by almost every student. What am I?
550. If you drop me, I’m sure to crack, but give me a smile and I’ll always smile back. What am I?
551. I am the start of every story and the root of every invention. I appear when the mind wanders. What am I?
552. I am the ladder with missing rungs but steady hands. What am I?
553. What has legs but doesn’t walk?
554. The more you take from me, the bigger I become. What am I?
555. What begins with P, ends with E, and has thousands of letters?
556. I am always ahead of you, yet you can never reach me. I’m your destination and your illusion. What am I?
557. What is black when you buy it, red when you use it, and gray when you throw it away?
558. I have keys but no locks. I have space but no room. You can enter but can’t go outside. What am I?
559. I can be found on walls, but I never hang. I hold numbers, but I don’t do math. I move hands, yet I have none of my own. What am I?
560. The more of me you take, the more you leave behind. What am I?
561. What has cities but no houses, forests but no trees, and rivers but no water?
562. I am the beginning of the end and the end of time and space. I’m essential to creation, and I surround every place. What am I?
563. I stretch across the world, yet I have no weight. I carry voices, messages, and even laughter. What am I?
564. You can hold me in your left hand, but never in your right hand. What am I?
565. What has keys but no locks, space but no room, and you can enter but not go inside?
566. I build up castles, I tear down mountains, I make some men blind, I help others to see. What am I?
567. I can bring two people closer together or tear them apart. I am spoken in haste and regretted in silence. What am I?
hint: They matter more than you think.

question: 568. What is seen in the middle of March and April that can’t be seen at the beginning or end of either month?
569. I can break without being dropped, and I can heal without being touched. I’m both strong and fragile. What am I?
570. I am the sound that can be felt but not heard. What am I?
hint: You feel me in your bones.

question: 571. You often see me when you least expect it, and I vanish when you try to focus on me. I’m a trick of the mind. What am I?
572. I am the mirror that shows the past in color. What am I?
573. What gets wetter the more it dries?
574. I am the bridge that crosses oceans of thought. What am I?
575. I am something that follows you throughout your entire life, but you can never see me in the mirror. What am I?
hint: Always with you, but disappears in the dark.

question: 576. The person who makes it has no need for it. The person who buys it doesn't use it. The person who uses it doesn't know it. What is it?
hint: It's only used once, usually not by choice.

question: 577. I can be carried everywhere, shared without effort, and yet I have no weight or shape. What am I?
578. I turn polar opposites into allies, and chaos into calculation. I’m found between disorder and design. What am I?
579. What comes once in a year, twice in a month, but never in a day?
580. I am the door that appears only when you knock inside. What am I?
581. I am always ahead of you, but you can never catch me no matter how fast you run. What am I?
582. I am the key that locks itself after use. What am I?
583. I never sleep but I always watch. I hold memories but never forget. I help you see, but I’m not alive. What am I?
hint: You carry me in your pocket now.

question: 584. I am the chorus that answers a single voice. What am I?
585. I start with a whisper and grow into a roar. I’m not alive, but I can destroy forests and silence cities. What am I?
586. What has hands but can not clap?
587. What has many keys but can not open a lock?
588. What has one eye but can not see?
589. What has legs but does not walk?
590. What kind of room has no doors or windows?
hint: It is a type of fungus.

question: 591. What runs around a backyard yet never moves?
592. What has an end but no beginning, a home but no family, and a space without a room?
593. What word is spelled wrong in every dictionary?
594. What building has the most stories?
595. If two is company and three is a crowd, what are four and five?
596. What is always in front of you but can not be seen?
597. What can travel all around the world without leaving its corner?
598. What has one head, one foot, and four legs?
599. What invention lets you look right through a wall?
600. What has words but never speaks out loud?
601. What can run but never walks, has a mouth but never talks?
602. What has many teeth but can not bite?
603. What can you hear but not see, touch, or smell?
604. What goes through cities and fields, but never moves?
605. What can be seen once in a year, twice in a week, and never in a day?
606. What starts with T, ends with T, and has T in it?
607. What is full of keys but cannot open a single lock?
608. What has lots of eyes but cannot see?
609. What has one eye and a long tail but no body?
610. What can go up a chimney down, but cannot go down a chimney up?
611. What has no life but can still die?
612. What has branches but no fruit, trunk, or leaves?
613. What can point in every direction but cannot reach the destination?
614. What has pages but is not a book?
615. What can you open but cannot close?
616. What has teeth on top but cannot chew?
617. What can be long or short, grown or bought, painted or left bare?
618. What goes from Z to A and still moves forward?
619. What can be measured but has no length, width, or height?
620. What can never be put in a saucepan?
621. What has a bed but never sleeps?
622. What can be opened by a key but is not a door?
623. What has many needles but does not sew?
624. What jumps when it walks and sits when it stands?
625. What has stripes but no color?
626. What can be stolen, mistaken, or changed, yet never leaves you your whole life?
627. What has roots that nobody sees, is taller than trees, up, up it goes, and yet never grows?
628. What can be swallowed but can also swallow you?
629. What can be touched but cannot be seen?
630. What has an eye at the center but cannot see?
631. What has four fingers and a thumb but is not a hand?
632. What has words on paper but no voice?
633. What can light a room without taking any space?
634. What has a face but no eyes, nose, or mouth?
635. What has a tail and a head but no body?
636. What can be written, spoken, and broken?
637. What can run around a track but never sweats?
638. What has one mouth but many ears?
639. What can be empty and still have weight?
640. What has a heart that does not beat?
641. What can you make that no one can see?
642. What has keys but no locks, space but no room, and you can enter but not go in?
643. What is black when clean and white when dirty?
644. What has holes on top and bottom but still holds water?
645. What has hands and a face but no body?
646. What can speak every language but has no mouth?
647. What has a lock but no key?
648. What can be long and short, fast and slow, but cannot be bought?
649. What can be counted but not seen?
650. What gets lost every time you stand up?
